name: County Fair Banana Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. For the crust: Chop pecans very fine (use a food processor with a metal blade). Mix with butter and sugar until well blended. Divide into two portions and press into the bottom of two pie pans. Chill for 30 minutes. Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes or until golden brown. Cool completely.
2. For chocolate layer: Melt chocolate, butter, and corn syrup in a double boiler over hot (not boiling) water. Stir frequently. Remove from heat and let cool. Stir in vanilla. Divide between cooled pie crusts and spread evenly.
3. For Filling: Scald milk in a medium saucepan over medium heat. In a bowl, beat egg yolks and gradually add sugar and flour until smooth. Gradually stir in hot milk. Cook in the saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until thickened (about 1 minute). Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in butter and vanilla. Cool to room temperature.
4. Whip cream until firm but not stiff. Mix a portion of the whipped cream into the cooled egg mixture for lightening. Fold in the remaining cream, bananas (peeled, sliced very thin, and tossed with lemon juice), and optional chopped pecans. Fill pastry shells evenly with banana cream.
5. Beat remaining cream until stiff. Spoon around the edges of the pies and sprinkle with additional chopped pecans if desired. Makes 2 pies.
